article_title: The 10 Laws of Insider Trading — So Far

description: Don't do it by buying short-dated out-of-the-money call options on merger targets.
article_title: The 10 Laws of Insider Trading — So Far

description: Don't text or email about it.
article_title: The 10 Laws of Insider Trading — So Far

description: Don't do it in your mother's account.
article_title: The 10 Laws of Insider Trading — So Far

description: Don't do it by planting bombs at a company and shorting its stock.
article_title: The 10 Laws of Insider Trading — So Far

description: Don't do it while employed at the Securities and Exchange Commission.
article_title: The 10 Laws of Insider Trading — So Far

description: Don't Google 'how to insider trade without getting caught' before doing it.
article_title: The 10 Laws of Insider Trading — So Far

description: If you didn't insider trade, don't forget and accidentally confess to insider trading.
article_title: The 10 Laws of Insider Trading — So Far

description: If you are going to insider trade, do it in a company that is far away from a Securities and Exchange Commission office. Like, physically.
article_title: The 10 Laws of Insider Trading — So Far

description: If you are already under a federal ethics investigation about your ownership or promotion of a stock, don't insider trade that stock.
article_title: The 10 Laws of Insider Trading — So Far

description: If you are planning to insider trade, probably don't keep a spreadsheet of the Money Stuff Laws of Insider Trading. That will definitely show up in the SEC's complaint against you.
article_title: The 10 Laws of Insider Trading — So Far

description: If you insider trade by buying short-dated out-of-the-money call options on a merger target, and the SEC freezes your profits, don't show up in a U.S. court to ask for them back.
If you are going to insider trade, or commit securities fraud generally, don’t talk loudly about it on the Acela from Washington to New York
